primary_catalyst.description: Tesla Q1 2026 results weighed on AI/growth complex: TSLA -3.59% to $373.60, lifted 2026 capex guide to $25B (+$5B) for AI/robotics and indicated negative FCF for rest of 2026. IBM -8%, ServiceNow -~18% on disappointing prints â€” broad software/tech sell-the-news day. S&P -0.42%, Nasdaq -0.89%. NVDA dragged with the group ahead of Intel's after-close earnings (which would drive the +4.32% Apr 24 move).

secondary_catalysts.description: IBM, ServiceNow Q1 misses dragged enterprise software; Lockheed -5%

force_attributions.rationale: Sell-the-news tape across enterprise software (IBM, NOW) dragged broader tech; NVDA as largest tech component got hit with the basket
IBM/NOW weak enterprise AI prints called enterprise-AI-adoption narrative into question temporarily
Iran ceasefire uncertainty still lurking in backdrop
TSLA capex raise to $25B could be read bullish A1 (more AI capex) but market read as 'negative FCF = growth-at-any-cost' â€” net negative for AI complex on the day

notes: MEDIUM priority. No NVDA-specific news â€” dragged by broader tech weakness. The setup for the Apr 24 Intel-driven +4.32% reversal.
